title: The Myth of the Right-Brained or Left-Brained Personality
subtitle: 左脑型还是右脑型？一个流行文化迷思的真相

paragraphs.en: There are hundreds of personality quizzes online that assert they can ascertain whether the right or left half of your brain is dominant. Left-brained people are supposedly logical and excel at language and math while right-brained people are more imaginative and emotionally intelligent. There's just one problem: That's not how brains work.
paragraphs.zh: 网上有数百种性格测验，声称能判断你是右脑主导还是左脑主导。左脑型的人据称逻辑性强，擅长语言和数学；而右脑型的人则更富想象力，情商更高。但问题只有一个：大脑并非如此运作。

paragraphs.en: Popular science enthusiasts sort of took this idea and ran with it, and it's become woven in popular culture now, and it's not going away. Despite this enduring belief, there's no such thing as being "right-brained" or "left-brained". Whether you're more creative or logical has nothing to do with one hemisphere being dominant over the other.
paragraphs.zh: 科普爱好者们对这个想法大做文章，如今它已深深融入流行文化，挥之不去。尽管这一信念根深蒂固，但根本不存在所谓的“右脑型”或“左脑型”人。你更偏创意还是更偏逻辑，与你哪一侧大脑半球占主导地位毫无关系。

paragraphs.en: But the actual science of how the two halves of our brains work together is sometimes stranger than fiction. The human brain is divided into two hemispheres, the left and right. In all vertebrate animals, the right hemisphere controls the left side of the body and vice versa.
paragraphs.zh: 但关于大脑两半球如何协同工作的真实科学，有时比虚构故事还要离奇。人脑分为左右两个半球。在所有脊椎动物中，右半球控制身体左侧，左半球控制右侧。

paragraphs.en: And scientists have long known, thanks to the behaviors of patients who suffered brain injuries, that different areas of the brain do different things. Work by neuroscientists has revealed the importance of different hemispheres for different activities. However, their research quickly saw some misinterpretations in the general public.
paragraphs.zh: 长期以来，科学家通过脑损伤患者的行为得知，大脑的不同区域负责不同功能。神经科学家的工作揭示了不同半球对不同活动的重要性。然而，他们的研究很快就在公众中遭到了一些误读。

paragraphs.en: Some presumed creative people must be right-brained and logical people left-brained. It is proven that not only is personality unrelated to the different halves of the brain, but people aren't really right- or left-brained to begin with. The idea that we have left-dominant and right-dominant people is categorically false.
paragraphs.zh: 一些人便据此推测，富有创造力的人必定是右脑型，逻辑性强的人则是左脑型。事实证明，不仅性格与大脑半球差异无关，而且人本身也并非真正的“右脑型”或“左脑型”。认为存在左脑主导型和右脑主导型人的观点是完全错误的。

paragraphs.en: Neuroscientists don't believe that and never have. What scientists learned is that there are really important differences between the left hemisphere and the right hemisphere. It's just that they have nothing to do with personality or whether cognitive strategy is more logical or creative.
paragraphs.zh: 神经科学家从不相信这一点，也从未相信过。科学家认识到的是，左半球和右半球之间确实存在重要差异，只不过这些差异与性格无关，也与认知策略是更偏向逻辑还是更偏向创意无关。

paragraphs.en: While researchers have shown the limitations of how the hemispheres influence our lives, it's not difficult to understand the appeal of such ideas. People are endlessly fascinated by themselves and their friends, and the subtle differences in how people think about the world are really meaningful to them.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然研究人员已证明了大脑半球对我们生活影响的局限性，但不难理解这类想法为何如此具有吸引力。人们对自己和朋友们有着无穷无尽的好奇，而人们看待世界方式的细微差异对他们来说意义重大。

paragraphs.en: When you come up with an online quiz that tells us something about ourselves, we're drawn to that. It's irresistible. But you have to take it with an enormous grain of salt.
paragraphs.zh: 当一个在线测验能告诉我们一些关于自己的事情时，我们就会被它吸引。这简直难以抗拒。但你必须对此抱有极大的怀疑态度。
